sections.title: A: Word formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: It's important to talk openly about your ...... . (FEEL)
Although he smiled, he actually felt very ...... . (HAPPY)
Teenagers often go through difficult ...... changes. (EMOTION)
Her teacher was very ...... and listened carefully to her problems. (SYMPATHY)
The students looked ...... during the long presentation. (BORE)
It was one of the most ...... games I've ever played. (EXCITE)
The children were really ...... about the school trip. (EXCITE)
My younger brother is always so ...... after drinking cola. (ENERGY)

sections.title: B: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Come ......! The film starts in five minutes.
Hang ...... a minute while I check my messages.
I wish those boys would shut ...... during the lesson.
The rain went ...... for hours and we couldn't go outside.
Please calm ...... and explain what happened.
Can you speak ...... a little? I can't hear you.
The dog ran ...... when the gate was left open.
My friends cheered me ...... after the bad result.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
I don't have enough money for concert tickets. **afford**
I ...... concert tickets.

Lisa acted happy, but she was actually upset. **pretended**
Lisa ...... happy, but she was actually upset.

The teacher forced us to apologise. **made**
We ...... apologise by the teacher.

I chose to speak to Jake about the problem. **decided**
I ...... to Jake about the problem.

In the end, we succeeded in making Anna smile. **managed**
We finally ...... Anna smile.

I try not to argue with my parents. **avoid**
I ...... with my parents.
She enjoys ...... detective novels.
They decided ...... abroad next year.
Both my cousins ...... my uncle live in Canada.
I don't like horror films, and ...... does my sister.
He stopped ...... when the teacher entered the room.
She tried ...... the answer, but it was too difficult.
Neither Emma ...... Chloe wanted to leave early.
I remember ...... that actor in another film.
'I'm really tired.' 'So ...... I.'
She forgot ...... her homework to school.
Either we leave now ...... we'll miss the train.
He admitted ...... the window by mistake.
'I love this song!' 'So ...... I!'
She refused ...... to the manager.

sections.title: E: Complete the sentences
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Everyone ...... out laughing when he slipped on the ice.
She burst into ...... after hearing the terrible news.
The audience was ...... with laughter during the comedy show.
He couldn't ...... laughing during the interview.
My grandfather ...... me a warm smile.
The little girl started ...... when she lost her toy.
Tears ...... down her cheeks as she spoke.
Jake ...... a funny joke at lunch.
She tried to keep a straight ...... but started laughing.
His friends cheered him ...... after the match.
The ending of the film moved everyone to ...... .
That comedian was hilarious — we were in ...... .
She tried ...... not to cry in front of everyone.
He couldn't ...... his laughter any longer.
